Davis VP2 pyranometers: ±3–5%.\n Ambi:
Davis VP2 pyranometers: ±3–5%.
    Ambient Weather stations: approximately ±15%.
    If you use consumer-grade equipment, tight threshold values may produce
    unreliable classifications during marginal sky conditions.
    Consider relaxing the thresholds by 0.05–0.10 for less precise sensors.
Decimal degrees. North is positive (e.g. 38.8894); South is negative (e.g. -33.8688).
Decimal degrees. West is negative (e.g. -77.0352); East is positive (e.g. 151.2093).
Default: enabled. When disabled, haze co:
Default: enabled. When disabled, haze conditions will not be reported
        regardless of PM2.5 levels.
Deletes all per-month baselines from the:
Deletes all per-month baselines from the API. The system will re-bootstrap
      as new clear-sky samples are collected. This cannot be undone.
Domain name is required for automated TL: Domain name is required for automated TLS.
Downloading… this may take a few minutes: Downloading… this may take a few minutes.
Downloads OpenStreetMap geographic featu:
Downloads OpenStreetMap geographic features (boundaries, roads, water) for the
    satellite map overlay. The data is stored as a PMTiles file on the API host and
    served to the dashboard on demand. Downloads may take several minutes depending
    on the station location and network speed.

Hygroscopic correction exponent (Hänel 1:
Hygroscopic correction exponent (Hänel 1976). Default 0.45 is suitable
        for most locations. Lower values reduce humidity sensitivity; higher values
        increase it.

LibreWxR provides high-quality radar (zo:
LibreWxR provides high-quality radar (zoom 12), 13 color schemes,

             or point to your own self-hosted instance for production reliability.

Open-Meteo computes both US and European: Open-Meteo computes both US and European AQI server-side.
OpenAQ provides PM2.5 and PM10 from gove:
OpenAQ provides PM2.5 and PM10 from government reference monitors
     in 141 countries. Observed data — eligible for haze confirmation.
     Data lag is approximately 1–2 hours; Aeris or IQAir are preferred
     when available. No regional scale configuration is required.
OpenStreetMap boundaries, roads, and wat: OpenStreetMap boundaries, roads, and water for the satellite map overlay.
OpenWeatherMap uses its own 1–5 air qual:
OpenWeatherMap uses its own 1–5 air quality scale.
     No regional configuration is required.

The database password and provider API k:
The database password and provider API keys are stored in the API's own secrets file on the weewx host.
      Do not commit any secrets file to version control.
The default display language for the das: The default display language for the dashboard. Operators and visitors can override this in the UI.
The forecast correction engine learns sy:
The forecast correction engine learns systematic temperature bias patterns
     at your station using Random Forest regression. It collects forecast-observation
     pairs, trains a model, and applies corrections to hourly forecast temperatures
     before serving them.
The haze detection calibration bootstrap:
The haze detection calibration bootstrap pulls historical PM2.5 data
           from
The haze detection system uses particula:
The haze detection system uses particulate matter (PM2.5) data and a
     hygroscopic scattering model to identify haze conditions. Calibration
     collects clear-sky solar transmittance samples month-by-month to establish
     a local seasonal baseline.
The proxy secret was accepted and a re-r: The proxy secret was accepted and a re-run session is open.
The sky cover classifier uses the Kasten:
The sky cover classifier uses the Kasten-Czeplak clearness index (K<sub>m</sub>) —
     the ratio of measured solar irradiance to the clear-sky theoretical maximum.
     Adjust the thresholds below to match your sensor's characteristics.
